reasoning: The flagged configuration (sanctuary:/tmp + blocking /bin/sh) is an upstream design decision, not a supply-chain attack. It doesn't execute during makepkg/build/install and falls outside AUR audit scope.
disagreement_analysis: qwen misclassified a runtime configuration risk as a supply-chain threat. Deepseek correctly focused on build/install execution risks.
coverage_issues: Both models skipped v4.4.0.tar.gz source verification
false_positives: qwen's voix.conf verdict: Runtime config misinterpreted as supply-chain threat (AUR scope is build/install execution)
learnings: Clarify audit scope: Emphasize runtime configs ≠ supply-chain risks unless executed during build/install
Improve binary handling: Prioritize checksum verification for skipped archives
Enhance configuration analysis: Train models to distinguish between security anti-patterns vs malicious payloads
